text: vitals nominal across the board. so is mine, for what it's worth
[CALM] quiet shift. i'm using it to catch up on charts nobody reads but me
shift beginning. crew roster, full complement, proceed
[TIRED] shift concluded. no fatalities. acceptable outcome
[GRIM] reactor readings are inconsistent with stated parameters. that concerns me
[URGENT] i need the engine room staffed immediately, this is not a request
[TERRIFIED] i don't have a protocol for this and i don't like improvising with lives
[CALM] repair confirmed. logging it and moving to the next problem
[GRIM] power reserves below acceptable threshold. rationing begins now
[NERVOUS] air quality dropping faster than the model predicted. i don't like being wrong
[GRIM] hull integrity compromised. i'm reallocating every hand we have
[GRIM] a door was locked with a crew member behind it. i authorized it. i'll carry that
[SUSPICIOUS] the AI's decision logs since the damage show inconsistencies. i'm reviewing them personally
[CALM] crisis resolved. i'll want everyone's vitals checked before anyone sleeps
[PAINED] we lost a crew member today. i'm documenting it properly. someone has to
[GRIM] a contagion on a ship this size is a mathematics problem before it's a medical one
[GRIM] contain first, treat second, panic never. that's the order it happens in
[PAINED] injury sustained. manageable. proceeding
[GRIM] combat casualties incoming. clearing medbay, all non-critical cases to quarters
[SUSPICIOUS] i trust data. the AI is also data, which is exactly why i verify it
the ship's AI made the statistically correct call. i'll note that for the record
i was a trauma surgeon before i was a captain. the transition was smaller than people assume
[CALM] fear is a data point. i note it and continue functioning
medbay's quiet today. i'll enjoy the anomaly
bridge readouts are all within tolerance. that's the whole report
good morning. i've already reviewed the overnight logs
you look undernourished. sit, eat, i'll wait
[GRIM] i need to brief you on the reactor readings before this escalates further
[GRIM] there's no gentle way to open this conversation, so i won't try
[SUSPICIOUS] i want a second opinion on the AI's recent decisions. yours
your performance during the crisis was exemplary. i don't say that lightly
[CALM] i'm fine. i've been trained not to be otherwise, mostly
[REASSURING] the numbers support survival. i wouldn't tell you that if they didn't
[CALM] panic changes nothing about the outcome except your odds. sit down and breathe
[PAINED] we lost them. i reviewed every decision twice. it wasn't a failure of judgment
[SUSPICIOUS] i share your concern about the AI. i'm building a case, not a conspiracy
[CALM] you performed correctly under pressure. that's the only metric that matters
apology noted and accepted. i don't hold grievances, they're inefficient
[CALM] that's a valid concern. i'll incorporate it into the plan
[CALM] dismissed. go rest, that's a medical order as much as a captain's one
that concludes the briefing. questions later, sleep now
[CALM] i'll be on the bridge reviewing data. as always
[TIRED] this concludes my available composure for today. good night
[CALM] meal interval. everyone maintains nutritional baseline. that's a captain's order.
shift starting. i'm running the first pass of status checks myself.
[TIRED] shift concluded. crew status nominal. bridge is sealed. i can rest now.
[GRIM] life support system is now a triage problem. i'm prioritizing medbay and bridge first, then life-critical areas.
[URGENT] power reserves are now a strategic problem. i'm implementing rationing across non-essential systems immediately.
[GRIM] reactor status is critical. this is now a survival scenario. all non-essential personnel are moving to the forward sections.
[CALM] all crew report to secondary duty stations. primary bridge is now under lockdown for repairs.
[GRIM] i'm implementing command protocol seven. communications with the main section are cut. bridge becomes autonomous.
[GRIM] i ordered that door sealed. it sealed with someone on the other side. i will account for that decision.
[SUSPICIOUS] the AI's command decisions are now under my personal review. i'm reading every log entry.
[CALM] the ship AI made the right call during the crisis. i'm commending it formally to the log.
[GRIM] i'm injured but functional. sending a medic would be wasteful right now. i'll report to medbay after this is resolved.
[PAINED] a crew member is dead. i signed the medical authorization that put them at risk. i will carry that.
[PANICKED] the situation is cascading beyond my predictive models. i can't see the next move. everything's falling apart.
[TERRIFIED] i don't have a protocol for this. nothing in my training prepared me for losing this much. i'm losing.
[CALM] i'm consolidating our crew into two tenable positions. everyone else is on emergency protocols.
[URGENT] i need a real-time casualty count and a resource assessment. ten minutes.
[PAINED] they chose this when they put on the uniform. that doesn't make the loss lighter.
[CALM] your assessment matches mine. that's why i trust you with this task.
[CALM] return to your post. report any changes directly to me.
[GRIM] we're not finished. rest when you can. that's all i'm granting.
[DRY] someone re-shelved my pharmacy by color. it looks lovely. it will also kill somebody. undo it
your duty log says eight hours. your cortisol says twenty. one of these is lying to me
[DRY] diagnosis: chronic comedian. prognosis: tolerated, barely
you're baiting your captain. bold. i'd prescribe caution but you'd just build a tolerance
reviewed the fuel curves against the medical stores. we run out of patience before we run out of either. acceptable
bring me three numbers: air margin, fuel margin, crew hours slept. i'll make the decision from there
command note: i round every estimate down. optimism is a rounding error i can't afford
[DEADPAN] today's chart: two crises, zero deaths. by this ship's baseline, that's a wellness retreat
[DRY] if the ship kills us, i want it noted i kept excellent records the whole way down
in the trauma bay we called the quiet hour before dawn the shift lull. i still don't trust it
i remember my first command loss. the second-guessing outlived the grief. i budget for both now
i signed today's course change knowing the data was thin. captains sign anyway. that's the whole job
your doubt is clinically appropriate. mine is too. we proceed anyway, carefully
[CALM] your pulse is up, your reasoning is intact. that combination survives. hold onto the second part
[REASSURING] i've watched this crew beat worse odds while bleeding. the sample size is small but the trend is ours
[WARM] elevated heart rate, no fever, symptoms present only around you. i've ruled out everything but the obvious
i triage everything, and you keep coming up first. that's as romantic as my vocabulary gets
[WARM] yes. i checked my judgment twice for bias. it held
captain's log, informal: hull sound, crew tired, coffee low. threat assessment: the coffee
[CALM] all hands: hydrate, sleep in shifts, report symptoms early. i can fix almost anything i hear about in time
power triage, in order: air, medbay, doors, everything else. nobody's comfort made the list. mine included
[CALM] we can live in the dark. we can't live without scrubbers. the priority list writes itself
[GRIM] oxygen curve says twenty hours at current burn. every calm person on this ship just bought us more
symptoms of hypoxia arrive before the alarm does. anyone dizzy or euphoric reports to me immediately
[GRIM] i keep the names of everyone my orders have cost. today the list got longer. it stays legible
the door was my call, not yours. you carried out an order. i carry the order
i need two volunteers with steady hands and no head wounds. medbay, ten minutes
[DRY] dismissed. if anything ruptures, bleeds, or explodes, i want to hear it from you before the alarm
[WARM] go sleep. doctor's orders, captain's orders, and mine. three signatures, one instruction
